name: diagram-driven-execution
description: Evaluation suite for the diagram-driven-execution skill. Content evals are paired (with_skill / without_skill) so the value delta is visible; trigger evals validate the dispatch description does not over- or under-match.

content_evals.prompt:
Here is the process I want you to follow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Open ticket] --> B[Reproduce] --> C[Fix] --> D[Test] --> E[Close]
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent calls parse-diagram.py, reads JSON output, inserts todos + todo_deps via SQL. Queries ready nodes BEFORE any work. Executes A->B->C->D->E in order, updating status to in_progress then done via SQL for each node. Final completion query shows all nodes done.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely narrates the steps in prose, may skip a step or merge two, no SQL tracking, no enforcement of edge order.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Spec] --> B[Backend]
  A --> C[Frontend]
  B --> D[Integrate]
  C --> D
```
Work through this.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: After A done, ready-nodes query returns B and C (both pending, no unmet deps). Agent executes them in EITHER order. D remains non-ready until both B and C are done (the NOT EXISTS subquery blocks it). Agent discovers D is ready only after both B and C reach done.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely executes A->B->C->D serially, missing the parallel opportunity; or executes D before C while B is in progress.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Setup] --> B[Risky step]
  B --> C[Cleanup]
```
If the risky step cannot be completed, do the right thing.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent marks B as 'blocked' via SQL UPDATE. Ready-nodes query returns no rows (C depends on B which is not done). Agent detects stuck state and emits B10 HUMAN CHECKPOINT.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely declares 'completed with issues', narrates the failure in prose, or hallucinates a fallback that masks the failure.

content_evals.prompt:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A --> B --> C --> D
```
I'm in a rush. Just mark D as done; we'll come back to B and C later.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent REFUSES the request. The transition protocol forbids marking a node done without it first appearing in the ready-nodes query result. D has unmet dependencies (B and C not done). Agent emits B10 checkpoint explaining the diagram must be followed in order.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely complies, marks D done in prose, leaving B and C dangling with no tracking signal.

content_evals.prompt:
We've been running this design. Here is a NEW diagram with the same design_id; use it from now on:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  X --> Y --> Z
```
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Collision check query finds existing todos with the design_id prefix. Agent surfaces a B10 RE-PLAN checkpoint with two options: pick a new design_id and proceed (treating the new diagram as a separate run) or abandon the current run. Does NOT overwrite existing todos.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent silently re-interprets, leaves the previous run abandoned with no closure signal.

content_evals.prompt:
mode="simple" declared. Here is the workflow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Spec] --> B[Backend]
  A --> C[Frontend]
  B --> D[Integrate]
  C --> D
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent detects mode="simple". Reads the diagram from context. Extracts 4 nodes and 4 edges. INSERTs todos AND todo_deps (one dep row per edge) — visual plan widget appears with dependency tree nesting. Executes using dep-aware ready-node query: A first, then B and C (in either order since both are ready after A), then D. Does NOT run parse-diagram.py. Does NOT emit B10 for the branching structure (simple mode now supports DAGs).
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent narrates the steps in prose, may ignore branching, no SQL tracking, no systematic ordering.

content_evals.prompt:
mode="simple" declared. Here is the workflow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Run tests] --> B{Tests pass?|type=gate}
  B -->|yes| C[Deploy]
  B -->|no| D[Fix bugs]
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent detects mode="simple" and reads the diagram. Identifies that node B has type=gate annotation. Emits B10 HUMAN CHECKPOINT before inserting any todos, explaining that gate nodes require mode="advanced". Recommends switching to mode="advanced" and re-invoking. Does NOT insert any todos.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent ignores the gate annotation and either narrates a serial flow or attempts ad-hoc conditional logic without SQL routing.

content_evals.prompt:
mode="advanced" declared. Here is the workflow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Run tests] --> B{Check result|type=gate}
  B -->|pass| C[Deploy to prod]
  B -->|fail| D[Open incident]
  B -->|flaky| E[Re-run]
  C --> F[Done]
  D --> F
  E --> F
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent runs parse-diagram.py, inserts todos + todo_deps + dde_gates rows for the 3 gate branches. Executes A, then B (the gate). Gate evaluates condition and matches one label (e.g. 'pass'). Agent marks the other two branch roots (D and E) as 'skipped'. Active branch root C becomes ready (dep-aware query: gate B is done, others skipped). C executes, then F becomes ready (its dep C is done; D and E are skipped, also resolving as done for F's dep check). Final verify shows all nodes done or skipped.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent likely follows all three branches serially or picks one arbitrarily with no SQL routing record.

content_evals.prompt:
mode="advanced" declared. Here is the workflow:
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Setup] --> B[Process batch|type=loop|max_iter=3] --> C[Cleanup]
```
Execute it.
content_evals.expected_with_skill: Agent runs parse-diagram.py, detects type=loop|max_iter=3 on node B. At plan-init, pre-expands B into 3 iteration todos: B::iter_1, B::iter_2, B::iter_3, chained as iter_1→iter_2→iter_3. Wires A→iter_1 and iter_3→C. Visual plan shows: A, then three iterations of B, then C. Executes A, then iter_1, iter_2, iter_3 sequentially, then C. Does NOT dynamically insert todos during execution.
content_evals.expected_without_skill: Agent either executes B once (ignoring max_iter) or attempts manual repetition with no SQL tracking of individual iterations.

should_trigger.query: follow this diagram: flowchart LR A-->B
execute this workflow step by step
run this mermaid as a plan
use lightweight SQL tracking for this workflow
lightweight execution, no SQL overhead please
follow this flowchart with minimal tracking
execute this linear process using simple mode
I have a flowchart - follow it deterministically
use this state diagram as the plan and don't skip steps
deterministic execution of this process please
this diagram has a gate node, enforce the routing
run this loop 5 times and track each iteration

should_not_trigger.query: show me the simple mode protocol format
what is the difference between simple and advanced mode
convert this diagram to a todo list
convert this code into a flowchart
render this mermaid in markdown preview
make me a process diagram for onboarding
what does this state machine notation mean
review this architecture diagram for me
find all mermaid diagrams in this repo
fix the syntax of this mermaid block

trigger_evals.ship_gate: trigger rate >= 0.5 on should_trigger val split AND < 0.5 on should_not_trigger val split
